  "findings": [
    "Auxiliary heat is locked on continuously during operation",
    "Potential short circuit in the W2/Aux control wiring or a welded/stuck sequencer relay contact",
    "Incorrect outdoor temperature sensor configuration or outdoor thermostat lock-out setpoint failure"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate power to the HVAC unit and thermostat",
    "Test for continuity across the auxiliary heat relay/sequencer contacts to check for a welded state",
    "Verify thermostat configuration settings, outdoor air sensor operation, and control wiring for shorts"
  ],
  "rationale": "The auxiliary heat being locked on indicates a control sequence failure, a shorted control wire (W1/W2 to R), or a failed/welded sequencer/relay. This causes excessive energy consumption and potential equipment overheating, which constitutes a failure of the operating asset.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer wiring diagrams and sequence of operations",
    "ASHRAE 90.1 Energy Standard for Buildings",
    "National Electrical Code (NEC) Class 2 Control Circuits"
  ]
}
